(2) g/dL: hemoglobin line 649. (3) %: hematocrit line 664. (4) mg/dL: calcium line 747, corrected_calcium line 762, magnesium line 819. (5) ng/dL: free_t4 line 602. changes.check: Source cross-ref — units.yaml missing conversions confirmed
changes.result: CONFIRMED. units.yaml contains NO conversion entries for: K/μL ↔ 10^9/L (lines 44–49 define K/μL unit but no conversion block), mg/dL ↔ mmol/L for calcium, mg/dL ↔ mmol/L for magnesium, ng/dL ↔ pmol/L for free_t4, mEq/L ↔ mmol/L for electrolytes. Conversion factors for these pairs are what LC-S8B must evaluate evidence for — they must NOT be invented in the policy doc.

CONFIRMED. Prompt correctly treats HbA1c as a dedicated policy decision requiring five explicit sub-decisions before any approval. Default decision is NEEDS_POLICY_DECISION unless fully evidenced. Dual-representation de-duplication, canonical direction, and harmonisation code validity must all be resolved in the document.

CONFIRMED. Prompt explicitly prohibits: 0.438 %, 0.438 with % label, value in fraction with reference range in percent, value in percent with reference range in fraction. Policy must separate analytical from display canonical.

changes.check: K/μL equivalence — evidence required before APPROVED_LABEL_EQUIVALENCE_ONLY
changes.result: CONFIRMED. Prompt requires validation of numerical equivalence before approving. From LC-S8A: 1 K/μL = 1 × 10^3/μL = 1 × 10^9/L (since μL = 10^-6 L → 10^3/μL = 10^3/10^-6 L = 10^9/L). This is mathematical equivalence. Evidence source must be cited in the policy document (e.g. SI unit definition, BCSH guidelines, UK NEQAS, or equivalent).

CONFIRMED. Prompt explicitly states these are high-risk true conversion areas and default decision is NEEDS_CONVERSION_FACTOR_VALIDATION unless conversion factors are fully evidence-validated. No conversion factors may be invented. This is correctly protective.

CONFIRMED INDEPENDENT. LC-S8A audit paper is available as an input reference (read in full). LC-S8B must not treat LC-S8A as implementation authority — prompt explicitly states this. Every policy row in the table must cite its own authoritative source. LC-S8A findings may inform which rows to evaluate but cannot be the sole evidence.

surfaced_risks.risk: LC-S8B policy document must not be treated as implementation authority. The required §13 non-authority warning must be present and explicit. Any downstream sprint (LC-S8C/D/E/F) that cites this document as authority without a fresh governed prompt is a SOP violation.
surfaced_risks.severity: GOVERNANCE — enforce via required document structure

surfaced_risks.risk: HbA1c dual-representation policy carries the highest downstream complexity. If the policy document casually folds hba1c into generic unit conversion or approves mmol/mol change without resolving all five sub-decisions, it will authorise a downstream sprint that would break scoring fixtures broadly.

Calcium/magnesium/free_t4 conversion factors must come from governed clinical references (NHS lab directory, NICE, clinical chemistry standards). The LC-S8A audit cited factors (0.2495/4.008, 0.4113/2.431, 12.87/0.0777) as illustrative but flagged confirmation required. The LC-S8B policy document must cite primary sources for these factors before any APPROVED_FOR_IMPLEMENTATION decision.

Haematocrit L/L vs % policy requires careful handling. UK labs report both formats. The policy must resolve whether L/L is treated as an accepted input alias or a distinct analytical canonical. An incoherent decision here (approving both without specifying the canonical direction) will produce the same value/range unit split that the LC-S8 scoring guard was designed to catch.
